description: The 2024 Taiwan presidential election resulted in Lai Ching-te of the DPP winning with 40.05% of the vote in a three-way race, but the scenario focuses on the support trajectory for the DPP's continuation of power against the KMT-led opposition, reflecting a pivot from the status quo.

key_events.description: Failure of potential opposition coalition talks between KMT and TPP.
Increased regional tensions influence voters' focus on security.
Final campaign rallies emphasize national identity.
Official vote count confirms DPP retains presidency.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling multi-party dynamics where the primary goal is understanding support for the incumbent party versus a fragmented opposition.
